runs.content:
ASML picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($1,843.04) — set the day before today's Q2 earnings beat (€9.33B revenue) and second FY26 guidance raise of the year (to €43–45B on AI demand), so entry captures the pre-announcement price. CSCO hit its 60-day max hold and closed flat (−0.95%); CRWD triggered T1 (+27.05%); IBM repriced sharply to −3.52% after yesterday's Q2 profit warning.

CleanSpark (CLSK) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (+2.9% gap) after a 20-year, $6.6B data-centre lease (Sandersville, GA, extendable to $11.6B) drove an elevated-volume +8.8% session on Jul 14 — the cleanest single-stock catalyst available versus the more diffuse semiconductor-sector rebound or the multi-day oil-refiner rally.

Caledonia Mining picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close as a deep-value small-cap gold miner (P/E ~6x, P/B ~1.4x) with BlackRock crossing major shareholding thresholds three days running and a gold-price tailwind from escalating Strait of Hormuz tensions; SKYT was stopped out (−18.39%) and TNET hit T1 (+26.79%) the same session.

No Portfolio 2 trade today — checked GS, BAC, WFC, BIIB, ST, CRCL, WDFC, SAIA, RTX and LMT but none cleared the ≥2x average-volume bar with an intact catalyst; AppLovin (APP) was stopped out (−19.52%) on a single volatile session amid a broader high-multiple tech de-rating.
